Off Calendar Jun-27-2019 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Melanie Emmons for Jewish Family & Children's Services.

Ms. Emmons stated that she has an order, that she is foregoing the request for the additional five hours of time for the Attorney and for the Trustee, and that they had to keep the estate open for tax liabilities. The Petition for Approval of Trustee's and Attorney's Fees, etc. is under submission.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for (trustee) Jewish Family & Children's Services.

Ms. Friedeberg stated that there were numerous assets to marshal, that they were working with the previous trustee which took time, that she has talked to the trustee regarding the inappropriate entries and that they were dealing with beneficiaries who had issues (dementia)and their public benefits. The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Mayo. (Not Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for petitioner Jewish Family & Children's Services.

Trisha Friedeberg reported that she had filed an amendment to the petition and waivers of notice on December 17, 2015. The Court announced that he would continue the matter briefly to give the Examiner time to review the new documents. The matter was continued to January 11, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Paul H. Alvarado. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Robles. (Not reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT
Off Calendar Nov-18-2015 Continued To Dec-21-15 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for (petitioner) Jewish Family & Children's Services.

The Court stated that this case has been languishing. Ms. Friedeberg reported that there were a number of issues that are now resolved, that 2 of the beneficiaries are on public benefits and 1 is mentally ill, that they needed to make sure that those beneficiaries had consulted with attorney's regarding their money and that all of the beneficiaries will sign waivers. The accounting petition was continued to December 21, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Kabasares. (Not Reported)

Petition To Bring Trust Uner Court Supervision And For Appointment Of Jewish Family & Children'S Services As Successor Trustee; For Issuance Of Bond..
Off Calendar Dec-17-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Trisha Friedeberg for Jewish Family and Children's Services ("JFCS"). Rebecca Paul from JFCS was also present.

The Court stated that it will allow a maximum of $700.00 for fees requested by the Petitioner, and that there is no notice on the issue of Christmas gifts to the Sister and the Niece. The Court granted the Petition to Bring Trust Under Court Supervision, etc. (subject to receipt and review of a revised order).
